# PUBLICATION_PROJECTION_MODEL

**Document ID:** CASE001-PUB-PROJECTION

**Title:** Publication — Projection Model

**Document Class:** Investigation Artifact

**Status:** Stable

**Author:** SvitloSk Project — Architectural Viewpoint Audit №001

---

# Purpose

This document constructs a projection model that explains all previous contradictions about Publication.

---

# Projection Model

## Canonical Publication

**Definition:** The platform-independent, layer-independent concept of "one unit of public information generated by SvitloSk for distribution to residents."

**Properties:**

- Has identity (internal, not platform-specific)
- Has content (territorial information about outages)
- Has lifecycle (Created → Distributed → Archived)
- Has owner (Publication Engine)
- Has relationships (belongs to Issue, contains Territory)
- Is deterministic (same input = same output)
- Is traceable (to official source data)

---

## Projection Diagram

```text
                    ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
                    │      CANONICAL PUBLICATION       │
                    │  (Platform-independent concept)  │
                    └───────────────┬─────────────────┘
                                    │
            ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐
            │                       │                       │
            ▼                       ▼                       ▼
    ┌───────────────┐      ┌───────────────┐      ┌───────────────┐
    │ Business View │      │ Domain View   │      │ Editorial View│
    │               │      │               │      │               │
    │ - Stakeholders│      │ - Identity    │      │ - Standards   │
    │ - Value       │      │ - Invariants  │      │ - Templates   │
    │ - Purpose     │      │ - Lifecycle   │      │ - Formatting  │
    └───────┬───────┘      └───────┬───────┘      └───────┬───────┘
            │                       │                       │
            └───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┘
                                    │
            ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐
            │                       │                       │
            ▼                       ▼                       ▼
    ┌───────────────┐      ┌───────────────┐      ┌───────────────┐
    │ Telegram View │      │ Storage View  │      │ Documentation │
    │               │      │               │      │               │
    │ - Message ID  │      │ - Database ID │      │ - Glossary    │
    │ - Bot API     │      │ - Persistence │      │ - Specifications│
    │ - Channel     │      │ - Archive     │      │ - Definitions │
    └───────────────┘      └───────────────┘      └───────────────┘
```

---

## View Definitions

### Business View

**Projects:** What Publication MEANS to the business

**Shows:**

- Stakeholders (residents, administrators)
- Value (timely information)
- Purpose (community service)
- Business rules (Editorial Policy)

**Hides:**

- Technical implementation
- Platform details
- Storage mechanisms

**Used by:** Business stakeholders, project charter

---

### Domain View

**Projects:** What Publication IS in the domain

**Shows:**

- Identity (internal ID)
- Invariants (deterministic, traceable)
- Lifecycle (Generated → Archived)
- Relationships (Issue, Territory, Template)

**Hides:**

- Platform-specific details
- Storage details
- Editorial details

**Used by:** Domain experts, architects

---

### Editorial View

**Projects:** How Publication follows editorial standards

**Shows:**

- Standards (Editorial Policy)
- Templates (Canonical Templates)
- Formatting (Formatting Rules)
- Territorial grammar

**Hides:**

- Technical implementation
- Platform details
- Storage details

**Used by:** Editors, content creators

---

### Telegram View

**Projects:** How Publication appears on Telegram

**Shows:**

- Telegram Message ID
- Bot API interaction
- Channel delivery
- Platform-specific formatting

**Hides:**

- Domain internals
- Storage details
- Business rules

**Used by:** Telegram integration, channel management

---

### Storage View

**Projects:** How Publication is persisted

**Shows:**

- Database ID
- Persistence mechanism
- Archive strategy
- Query patterns

**Hides:**

- Domain internals
- Platform details
- Business rules

**Used by:** Data storage, persistence layer

---

### Documentation View

**Projects:** How Publication is documented

**Shows:**

- Glossary definitions
- Specifications
- Terminology
- Normative requirements

**Hides:**

- Implementation details
- Runtime behavior

**Used by:** Documentation, governance, reviews

---

# Contradiction Resolution

## How the Projection Model Resolves Contradictions

| # | Contradiction | How Projection Resolves |
|---|---------------|------------------------|
| 1 | GLOSSARY says "message"; TELEGRAM_GLOSSARY says "unit" | "Message" is the Telegram View; "unit" is the Domain View |
| 2 | Legacy TJS-002 has "Scheduled" state; TJS-021 does not | "Scheduled" was a view-specific state that was removed |
| 3 | Publication Engine owns; Publisher owns | "Publication Engine" is the Component View; "Publisher" is the Role View |
| 4 | Telegram Message ID is identity; internal ID is identity | Telegram Message ID is the Telegram View; internal ID is the Domain View |
| 5 | Platform-independent; Telegram-specific | Canonical Publication is platform-independent; Telegram View is platform-specific |
| 6 | Updated always returns to Published | Updated is a transient view-specific state; Canonical lifecycle is clearer |
| 7 | Archival trigger vague | Canonical Publication has clear archival rules; views implement them |
| 8 | Manual Publications excluded | Manual Publications are a different Canonical concept |
| 9 | Graphic Publications undefined lifecycle | Graphic Publications are a different Canonical concept |
| 10 | Repository vs Telegram authority | Canonical Publication is the authority; Repository and Telegram are views |
| 11 | Circular dependency with Issue | Canonical Publication belongs to Canonical Issue; views project differently |
| 12 | Identity undefined | Canonical Publication has internal identity; views project it |
| 13 | Ownership boundaries unclear | Canonical Publication has clear owner; views project ownership |
| 14 | Temporal scope vague | Canonical Publication has clear temporal rules; views implement them |
| 15 | Multi-channel publication unclear | Canonical Publication can be projected through multiple channel views |
| 16 | Publication granularity unclear | Canonical Publication has clear granularity; views transform it |

---

## Resolution Summary

| Status | Count |
|--------|-------|
| Resolved by projection | 16 |
| Unresolved | 0 |
| **Total contradictions** | **16** |

**ALL 16 contradictions are resolved by the projection model.**

---

# Projection Model Properties

## Property 1: Platform Independence

**Canonical Publication is platform-independent.**

Evidence:

- TJS-000 §3: "Telegram itself SHALL NOT be considered the Journal"
- TJS-000 §3: "The Journal exists independently from any publication platform"
- GLOSSARY.md: "Publication Channel — Examples: Telegram Journal; future mobile application"

**The Telegram View is platform-specific. The Canonical Publication is not.**

---

## Property 2: Layer Independence

**Canonical Publication is layer-independent.**

Evidence:

- Publication maintains identity across all layers (Identity Continuity Analysis)
- Publication has the same content across all layers
- Publication has the same lifecycle across all layers

**Each layer projects a different VIEW of the same Canonical Publication.**

---

## Property 3: DDD Neutrality

**Canonical Publication is DDD-neutral.**

Evidence:

- DDD classifications are analytical lenses, not alternative identities
- Aggregate Root, Entity, Value Object, Domain Event describe different ASPECTS
- They do not contradict each other

**DDD classifications are VIEWS of the Canonical Publication, not different objects.**

---

## Property 4: Semantic Consistency

**Canonical Publication provides semantic consistency.**

Evidence:

- All 7 definitions (GLOSSARY, TELEGRAM_GLOSSARY, DATA_MODEL, etc.) are view-specific projections
- They do not contradict each other; they describe different aspects
- Canonical Publication unifies them

**Canonical Publication is the SINGLE CONCEPT behind all definitions.**

---

# End of Projection Model
